name: DLR
description: Discrete Linear State-Space - represents linear time-invariant systems in discrete state-space form
xmlExample: <BasicBlock id="..." parent="..." interfaceFunctionName="DLR" blockType="d" dependsOnU="0" dependsOnT="0" simulationFunctionName="dsslti4" simulationFunctionType="C_OR_FORTRAN" style="DLR"><ScilabString as="exprs" height="2" width="1"><data line="0" column="0" value="1"/><data line="1" column="0" value="1+z"/></ScilabString><ScilabDouble as="realParameters" height="1" width="4"><data line="0" column="0" realPart="-1.0"/><data line="0" column="1" realPart="1.0"/><data line="0" column="2" realPart="1.0"/><data line="0" column="3" realPart="0.0"/></ScilabDouble><ScilabInteger as="nbZerosCrossing" height="1" width="1" intPrecision="sci_int32"><data line="0" column="0" value="0"/></ScilabInteger><ScilabInteger as="nmode" height="1" width="1" intPrecision="sci_int32"><data line="0" column="0" value="0"/></ScilabInteger><ScilabDouble as="dState" height="1" width="1"><data line="0" column="0" realPart="0.0"/></ScilabDouble><mxGeometry as="geometry" x="0.0" y="-10.0" width="60.0" height="60.0"/></BasicBlock>
anomalies: Requires event input for time stepping
criticalRules: State space matrices (A,B,C,D) are linearized in realParameters
